sections.paragraph: a root not used as a verb, to be full of food, to be well nourished, to be fat. Arab. مَرَأَ and مَرَِ bene profecit s. bene cessit cibus, مَرُوءَ to be strong (pr. fat), to be manly, whence مَرءُ Ch. מָרְא a man. Hence מָרִיא fat, מֻרְאָה the crop of a bird, and pr.n. מַמְרֵא. Very nearly kindred is בָּרָא Hiph. to fatten, בָּרִיא fat, which are referred above, page 138, B , to the notion of cutting, hence of eating. But perhaps it should rather be from the notion of filling, so that מָרֵא, בָּרֵא would nearly approach מָלֵא, which see. To this answer the Sanscrit prî, pri, to fill, to nourish, to sustain.
